answer:
**Charles Comte argues this in *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, Chapter 15 ("Of physical pains and pleasures considered as elements of the power of laws. — Of the judgments that have been passed on pleasures and pains of this kind, by religious sects and by philosophical sects").**[^1]

Comte contends that the Stoics' "contempt for physical pleasures and pains" was no abstract metaphysical doctrine but a practical response to the brutal sociological realities of ancient Rome and Greece: constant threats of proscription, confiscation of property, exile, enslavement of families, and death under tyrants like Marius, Sulla, the triumvirs, Tiberius, and Nero.[^2] These conditions made all evils "plausible," demanding maxims of resignation and preparation for inevitable calamity—doctrines suited to "enslaved men" that persisted across "savages," slaves, and subjects of despotism.[^3]

The specific line drawing the comparison is: "The maxims of Epictetus would suit **a slave of our modern colonists** no less than a subject of Nero."[^4]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/15-des-peines-et-des-plaisirs-physiques-consideres-co/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/15-des-peines-et-des-plaisirs-physiques-consideres-co/translation/).

[^2]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/15-des-peines-et-des-plaisirs-physiques-consideres-co/translation/#p-92f21f](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/15-des-peines-et-des-plaisirs-physiques-consideres-co/translation/#p-92f21f); [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/15-des-peines-et-des-plaisirs-physiques-consideres-co/translation/#p-8af7c1](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/15-des-peines-et-des-plaisirs-physiques-consideres-co/translation/#p-8af7c1).

[^3]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/15-des-peines-et-des-plaisirs-physiques-consideres-co/translation/#p-73b670](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/15-des-peines-et-des-plaisirs-physiques-consideres-co/translation/#p-73b670); [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/15-des-peines-et-des-plaisirs-physiques-consideres-co/translation/#p-733460](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/15-des-peines-et-des-plaisirs-physiques-consideres-co/translation/#p-733460).

[^4]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL I*, [/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/15-des-peines-et-des-plaisirs-physiques-consideres-co/translation/#p-73b670](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-i-7e62a2/15-des-peines-et-des-plaisirs-physiques-consideres-co/translation/#p-73b670).
